metadata.title: USCIS Naturalization English Test Practice
metadata.description: Practice content for the three components of the USCIS English test: Reading, Writing, and Speaking. Based on official USCIS vocabulary lists and test format.

reading.sentence: Abraham Lincoln was the President during the Civil War.
George Washington was the first President.
George Washington is the Father of Our Country.
The President lives in the White House.
Congress is in the capital of the United States.
People vote for the President.
Citizens can vote for the President.
People in America can vote.
The American flag is red, white, and blue.
The colors of the flag are red, white, and blue.
Congress elects the President of the United States.
People elects Congress.
We have Senators in Congress.
George Washington is on the dollar bill.
Abraham Lincoln was the most famous President.
The United States has many states.
America is a large country.
What state do you live in?
Where does the President live?
When is Independence Day?
Independence Day is in July.
When is Memorial Day?
When is Flag Day?
When is Labor Day?
When is Thanksgiving?
Columbus Day is in October.
Presidents' Day is in February.
Who was the first President of the United States?
What is the largest state in America?
Citizens have the right to vote.
People come to America for many reasons.
What are the colors of the American flag?
How many states are in the United States?
Who elects Congress?
Why do people want to come to America?
The Bill of Rights is for the people.
We pay taxes to the government.
Where is the capital of the United States?
What city is the capital of the United States?
The United States government is for the people.
Who lived in America first?
We have many rights in America.
The flag of the United States has many colors.
People in the U.S. can vote for President.
Who was Abraham Lincoln?
What does the President do?
How many Senators does Congress have?
The second President of the United States was Adams.
What is one right in the Bill of Rights?
The flag has red and white and blue.
Congress is in Washington.
Who can vote in the United States?
Where does Congress meet?
Citizens have many rights in the United States.
The largest state is in the north.

writing.sentence: Citizens can vote for President.
Washington is the capital of the United States.
Abraham Lincoln was President during the Civil War.
The President lives in the White House.
George Washington was the first President.
The American flag is red, white, and blue.
Citizens have the right to vote.
Congress meets in Washington, D.C.
Alaska is the largest state.
Delaware was the first state.
The United States flag has stars and stripes.
People in the United States pay taxes.
Washington, D.C. is the capital of the United States.
Lincoln was the President during the Civil War.
Citizens have freedom of speech.
People can vote for Senators.
Adams was the second President.
Washington is the Father of Our Country.
Independence Day is in July.
Flag Day is in June.
Memorial Day is in May.
Presidents' Day is in February.
Labor Day is in September.
Columbus Day is in October.
Thanksgiving is in November.
Canada is north of the United States.
Mexico is south of the United States.
New York City is in New York state.
California is a state in the United States.
The White House is in Washington, D.C.
Congress has Senators.
People elect Congress.
Citizens can vote for the President.
We have freedom of speech in the United States.
American Indians lived here first.
People want to be free.
People come to the United States to be free.
Washington lived in the United States.
The United States has many states.
Citizens have many rights.
One right is freedom of speech.
The flag of the United States is red, white, and blue.
People pay taxes to the United States.
Washington was the first President of the United States.
The United States is a free country.
We elect Senators to Congress.
The President can come from any state.
California has many people.
Alaska is the largest state in the United States.
Lincoln was a President of the United States.
The Civil War was in the United States.
Washington is on the dollar bill.
Citizens can elect the President.
We have many Senators in Congress.
The United States has a flag.

speaking.prompt: What is your full name?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My full name is [first] [middle] [last].

speaking.prompt: What is your date of birth?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My date of birth is [month] [day], [year].

speaking.prompt: Where were you born?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I was born in [city], [country].

speaking.prompt: What is your country of nationality?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My country of nationality is [country].

speaking.prompt: What is your current home address?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My address is [street address], [city], [state] [zip code].

speaking.prompt: How long have you lived at your current address?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I have lived there for [number] years.

speaking.prompt: Are you married?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, I am married. / No, I am not married.

speaking.prompt: What is your spouse's name?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My spouse's name is [name].

speaking.prompt: Is your spouse a United States citizen?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, my spouse is a U.S. citizen. / No, my spouse is not a U.S. citizen.

speaking.prompt: How many children do you have?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I have [number] children.

speaking.prompt: What is your telephone number?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My telephone number is [number].

speaking.prompt: Do you work? What is your job?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, I work as a [job title].

speaking.prompt: Where do you work?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I work at [company name] in [city].

speaking.prompt: How long have you been a permanent resident?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I have been a permanent resident for [number] years.

speaking.prompt: When did you become a permanent resident?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I became a permanent resident on [date].

speaking.prompt: Have you traveled outside the United States?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, I traveled to [country] for [number] days. / No, I have not traveled outside the United States.

speaking.prompt: Why do you want to become a United States citizen?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I want to become a citizen because I love this country and want to vote and participate in the government.

speaking.prompt: Do you promise to tell the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, I do.

speaking.prompt: Do you understand what an oath is?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, an oath is a promise to tell the truth.

speaking.prompt: Have you ever been arrested or committed a crime?
speaking.sampleAnswer: No, I have never been arrested or committed a crime.

speaking.prompt: Have you ever claimed to be a United States citizen?
speaking.sampleAnswer: No, I have never claimed to be a United States citizen.

speaking.prompt: Have you ever voted in a United States election?
speaking.sampleAnswer: No, I have never voted in a United States election.

speaking.prompt: Do you pay your taxes every year?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, I pay my taxes every year.

speaking.prompt: Do you support the Constitution of the United States?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, I support the Constitution of the United States.

speaking.prompt: Are you willing to take the full oath of allegiance to the United States?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, I am willing to take the full oath of allegiance.

speaking.prompt: If the law requires it, are you willing to serve in the United States military?
speaking.sampleAnswer: Yes, I am willing to serve in the United States military if the law requires it.

speaking.prompt: Have you ever been a member of a group or organization?
speaking.sampleAnswer: No, I have not. / Yes, I was a member of [organization].

speaking.prompt: What is your race or ethnicity?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I am [race/ethnicity].

speaking.prompt: What is your height and weight?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I am [height] tall and I weigh [weight].

speaking.prompt: What is your email address?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My email address is [email].

speaking.prompt: Have you ever used a different name?
speaking.sampleAnswer: No, I have always used my current name. / Yes, I used the name [name] before.

speaking.prompt: How did you get your green card?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I got my green card through [family/employment/other].

speaking.prompt: Do you have any trips outside the United States planned?
speaking.sampleAnswer: No, I do not have any trips planned. / Yes, I plan to travel to [country].

speaking.prompt: What school did your children attend?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My children attend [school name] in [city].

speaking.prompt: How are you today?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I am fine, thank you.

speaking.prompt: Can you explain what you do at your job?
speaking.sampleAnswer: At my job, I [description of duties].

speaking.prompt: Who lives with you at your home?
speaking.sampleAnswer: I live with my [spouse/children/parents].

speaking.prompt: What is your Social Security number?
speaking.sampleAnswer: My Social Security number is [number].

speaking.prompt: Have you ever failed to file your taxes?
speaking.sampleAnswer: No, I have always filed my taxes on time.

speaking.prompt: Do you owe any taxes to the government?
speaking.sampleAnswer: No, I do not owe any taxes. / Yes, I have a payment plan for [amount].
